content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
John Bakker 
Dublin City Attorney 
Meyers -Nave 
555 I 2'h Street, Suite 1500 
Oakland, CA 94607 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-038 
Dear Mr. Bakker: 
March 15, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Dublin Vice Mayor Abe Gupta 
regarding his duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Refonn Act (the 
"Act").1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the 
Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of 
interest or Section I 090. 
QUESTION 
If Vice Mayor Gupta has a conflict of interest in decisions related to the Grafton Plaza 
Project (the "project"), may the city segment the project so that the Vice Mayor may participate in 
project decisions related to the hotel component of the project? 
CONCLUSION 
The decisions may be segmented. However, the Vice Mayor may only participate in the 
hotel decisions after the project decisions for which he has a conflict are made. Until the other 
project decisions are made with finality, without the Vice Mayor's participation, he may participate 
in any other segmented decisions. Thus, in your proposed segmentation plan the Vice Mayor may 
only participate in Item 4. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated, The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


FACTS 
File No. A-16-038 
Page No. 2 
The Grafton Plaza Project. Grafton Plaza is a 12.23-acre vacant site in Eastern Dublin. The 
General Plan allows it to be developed as either Campus Office or as Mixed Use. The City Council 
approved an ordinance in 2010 adopting a Stage 1 Planned Development Plan ("Stage 1 PD") that 
allows the developer to choose either Mixed Use (Option 1) or Campus Office (Option 2). The 
Stage 1 PD is the initial phase of the zoning action that the City Council must take under the terms 
of the City's Planned Development Zoning ordinance. It established the permitted uses under the 
mixed-use category (including certain types of residential, hotels, retail, and office), a generalized 
site plan, and the proposed densities. 
The final phase of the zoning action required before the site can be developed is the Stage 2 
Planned Development Plan ("Stage 2 PD"). The Stage 2 PD includes more detailed items, such as a 
detailed site plan, development regulations (setbacks, maximum lot coverage, height limits, parking 
requirements, etc.), and architectural standards. Like the Stage 1 PD, the City Council must adopt 
an ordinance approving the Stage 2 PD. 
The property owner has proposed a Stage 2 PD to implement its proposed project using 
Option 1 (Mixed Use). The proposed Stage 2 PD includes a site plan that depicts three separate 
components: 6.55 acres of residential; 2.00 acres for a hotel; and 3.68 acres of retail/office. The 
Stage 2 PD includes separate design regulations and architectural standards for the three 
components of the project. 
The property owner has applied for a vesting tentative map to create three parcels, one for 
each of the three components described in the PD. The City Council will be asked to approve the 
vesting tentative map. In conjunction with the property owner's application, the proposed 
developers of the hotel and residential sites have each submitted site development review 
applications for the hotel and residential components, respectively. The residential developer has 
also submitted an application to create residential lots on the residential site. Site development 
review involves, among other things, the City's consideration of the design of the proposed 
structures and of the site. The City Council will be asked to approve the site development review 
for the hotel and for the residential. Site development review is not proposed at this time for the 
retail/office component of the project. 
Vice Mayor Gupta 's Fi11a11cial Interest. Vice Mayor Gupta has a financial interest a 
condominium unit that is within 500 feet of the boundary of the Grafton Plaza project. He does not 
currently live in the unit, and it is occupied by a tenant. The unit in which Mr. Gupta has a financial 
interest is more than 500 feet from the hotel component of the project. Mr. Gupta intends to 
participate in the City Council public hearing on the project as a member of the general public 
under Regulation 18704( d)(2). 
You stated that Stage 2 PD has various components. 
• The proposed Stage 2 PD includes development regulations and architectural standards for 
each of the three sites. Thus, you believe the decisions are readily segmentable. 


File No. A-16-038 
Page No. 3 
• Other aspects of the Stage 2 PD, however, such as the detailed site plan, general plan 
consistency, compatibility with the Stage 1 PD, the phasing plan, and densities, relate to the 
entire site or at least are "inextricably interrelated" to the hotel site. The vesting tentative 
map, since it relates to the entire site, is not segmentable. The site development review 
approvals for the hotel and the residential, being related exclusively to individual sites, are 
already segmented. 
You ask whether the City may segment the project in the following order: 
• Item 1. Stage 2 PD excepting the development regulations and architectural 
standards for the hotel. Vice Mayor Gupta would recuse himself from the decision 
and only participate as a member of the general public under Regulation 
18704( d)(2). 
• Item 2. Stage 2 PD development regulations and architectural standards for the hotel. 
Vice Mayor Gupta would participate in his official capacity. 
• Item 3. Vesting tentative map for the site, site development review approval for the 
residential, and vesting tentative map for the residential lots. Vice Mayor Gupta 
would recuse himself from the decision and only participate as a member of the 
general public under Regulation l 8704(d)(2). 
• Item 4. Site development review approval for the hotel. Vice Mayor Gupta would 
participate in his official capacity. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using 
his or her position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. 
(Section 87103.) Financial interests from which a conflict of interest may arise are set forth in 
Section 871 03. They are, in pertinent part: 
• Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
$2,000 or more. 
• Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth $2,000 
or more. 
• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the 
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
• Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 


File No. A-16-038 
Page No. 4 
The Vice Mayor has an interest in his real property under Section 87103(b ). In addition, the Vice 
Mayor has an interest in a business (his rental business) presumably worth $2,000 or more. And he 
also has an interest in a source of income, his tenant, which presumably aggregates to $500 or more 
in the 12 months prior to the decision. (Section 87103(c).) 
However, a conflict of interest may arise only when the reasonably foreseeable financial 
effect of a governmental decision on the official's interests are foreseeable and material. Generally, 
if the interest is "not explicitly involved" in the decision, such as is the case with your facts, the 
financial effect is reasonably foreseeable if the effect can be recognized as a realistic possibility and 
more than hypothetical or theoretical. Moreover, the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a 
governmental decision on real property is material whenever the governmental decision affects real 
property located within 500 feet of the property line of the official's property unless the 
Commission determines that there are sufficient facts to indicate that there will be no reasonably 
foreseeable measurable impact on the official's property. (Regulation 18702.2(a)( 11 ).) 
You have not provided information for us to analyze whether there is a foreseeable and 
material financial effect on the Vice Mayor and his interests. You ask, however, assuming there is a 
conflict of interest whether the decisions may be segmented. 
part: 
Regulation 18706 permits an agency to segment certain decisions and provides in pertinent 
"(I) The decision in which the official has a financial interest can be 
broken down into separate decisions that are not inextricably interrelated to the 
decision in which the official has a disqualifying financial interest; 
"(2) The decision in which the official has a financial interest is segmented 
from the other decisions; 
"(3) The decision in which the official has a financial interest is 
considered first and a final decision is reached by the agency without the 
disqualified official's participation in any way; and 
"(4) Once the decision in which the official has a financial interest has 
been made, the disqualified public official's participation does not result in a 
reopening of, or otherwise financially affect, the decision from which the official 
was disqualified." 
It appears that the hotel aspects of the project, which are discrete in relationship to the other 
components, can be segmented from other decisions related to the project affecting property within 
500 feet of the Vice Mayor's property. 
However, Regulation l 8706(a)(3) provides that the decision in which the official has a 
financial interest 11111st be co11sidered first a11d a fi11a/ decisio11 is reached by tlie agency without 
the disqualified official's participation in any way. Consequently, Vice Mayor could only 
participate in the hotel decision Item 4, after all the project decisions for which he has a conflict are 


File No. A-16-038 
Page No. 5 
made. Until the other project decisions are made with finality (including item 3), without the Vice 
Mayor's participation, he may not participate in other segmented decisions. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
nt General Counsel 
ivision

in any other segmented decisions. Thus, in your proposed segmentation plan the Vice Mayor may 
only participate in Item 4.
